examples.english: I stayed at home all weekend.
examples.chinese: 我整個週末都待在家裡。

home.english: the place where you were born or feel you belong

examples.english: Taipei will always be my home.

home.english: to or at the place where you live
connected with the home or family

field.english: an area of land used for crops or animals

examples.english: The cows are in the field.

field.english: an area of study, work, or activity

examples.chinese: 醫學是一個艱深的領域。

field.english: an area where a sport is played

examples.english: The players ran onto the field.

field.english: to send out a team, candidate, or representative

examples.english: The party will field two candidates.
examples.chinese: 該黨會派出兩名候選人。

stand.english: to be upright on your feet
to accept or bear something unpleasant

examples.english: She took a clear stand on the issue.
examples.chinese: 她對這個議題表明了清楚的立場。

stand.english: a thing used to support or display something

examples.english: Put the phone on the stand.
He stayed true to his family.

voice.english: the sound produced when a person speaks or sings
the right or ability to express an opinion

examples.english: Students should have a voice in the decision.
examples.chinese: 學生應該在決定中有發言權。

voice.english: to express a feeling or opinion in words

cut.english: to divide or damage something with a sharp tool

examples.english: She cut the bread with a knife.

cut.english: to reduce the amount or size of something
an injury or opening made by something sharp

examples.english: He had a small cut on his hand.
examples.chinese: 他的手上有一個小傷口。

cut.english: a reduction in amount or size

fast.english: to eat no food for a period of time

examples.english: Some people fast for health reasons.
examples.chinese: 有些人為了健康而禁食。

fast.english: a period when you do not eat food

examples.english: He broke his fast at sunset.
examples.chinese: 他在日落時結束禁食期。

fight.english: to use physical force against someone
to try hard to stop or achieve something

examples.chinese: 他們為平等權利而抗爭。

examples.english: A fight broke out outside the bar.
examples.chinese: 酒吧外發生了一場打架。

love.english: a strong feeling of deep affection
to feel deep affection for someone or like something very much

open.english: not closed or available to use
to move something so it is no longer closed
an open or public place or situation

examples.english: They discussed the plan in the open.

side.english: one of the surfaces or parts away from the middle

examples.english: Write your name on the side of the box.
examples.chinese: 把你的名字寫在盒子的側面。

side.english: one of two or more groups in a dispute or game
at or on the side; additional

act.english: to perform a role in a play or film

examples.english: Helping him was a kind act.
examples.chinese: 幫助他是一個善良的行為。

act.english: a law passed by a government

bit.english: a small amount or piece of something

examples.english: There were bits of glass on the floor.

bit.english: the smallest unit of computer information

examples.chinese: 一個位元組有八個位元。

great.english: large in amount, size, or degree

examples.english: There was great interest in the project.
examples.chinese: 大家對這個專案有很大的興趣。
我們度過了很好的時光。

examples.english: The cup fell to the ground.

ground.english: a reason or basis for something

examples.english: There are no grounds for complaint.

ground.english: to stop a plane from flying or a child from going out

examples.chinese: 暴風雨使所有航班停飛。
我們看了一個喜劇節目。

amount.english: a quantity or sum of something

examples.english: A large amount of money was missing.

amount.english: to add up to or be equal to

bear.english: to accept or endure something difficult

examples.english: She could not bear the pain.

bear.english: to give birth to or produce

examples.english: The tree bears fruit in summer.
examples.chinese: 這棵樹在夏天結出果實。

bear.english: a large strong wild animal with thick fur

examples.english: It took a great deal of time.

deal.english: to take action about a problem or situation

examples.english: We must deal with this problem.
examples.chinese: 我們必須處理這個問題。

hard.english: firm and difficult to bend or break
with a lot of effort or force

examples.chinese: 每位家長都收到一封信。

examples.english: The parent company is in Japan.

state.english: the condition that someone or something is in

examples.english: The room was in a terrible state.

state.english: a political unit within a country
to say or write something clearly

turn.english: to move around a point or change direction
to become or make something become different

examples.english: The leaves turn yellow in autumn.

turn.english: a chance or time to do something

examples.english: It is your turn to speak.

catch.english: to stop and hold something moving
to be in time for a bus, train, or event

examples.chinese: 我們趕上了最後一班火車。

catch.english: something caught or the act of catching

check.english: to examine something to make sure it is correct or safe

examples.english: Do a quick check before you leave.
examples.chinese: 離開前做一次快速檢查。

check.english: a restaurant bill or a written order to pay money

examples.english: Can we have the check, please?

clear.english: without clouds, dirt, or objects blocking the view
to remove things from a place

front.english: the part of something that faces forward
to face a particular direction or place

join.english: to become a member of a group
to put or bring things together

examples.english: Join the two pieces of wood.

meet.english: to see and speak to someone
to satisfy a need or condition

examples.chinese: 這個計畫滿足我們的需要。

examples.english: That was real nice of you.
Write your name at the top.

top.english: highest in position, rank, or quality
to be higher or better than something

wrong.english: not suitable or not working properly

examples.english: Something is wrong with my phone.

examples.chinese: 你把我的名字拼錯誤地。

age.english: the number of years someone has lived or something has existed

examples.english: We live in the digital age.

age.english: to become older or make something look older

examples.chinese: 這張照片老化得很嚴重。

away.english: to or at a different place
at a distance in space or time

examples.english: The station is two miles away.

complete.english: to finish doing or making something

force.english: an organized group, especially military or police
to make someone do something against their will

form.english: a document with spaces for information
the shape, type, or structure of something

examples.english: Water can take the form of ice.

hold.english: to keep something in your hand or arms
to organize an event or meeting

examples.english: They will hold a meeting tomorrow.

hold.english: the act or way of holding something

examples.english: Keep a firm hold on the rope.

job.english: paid work or a position of employment
a task or piece of work

examples.english: Cleaning the garage is a big job.
examples.chinese: 清理車庫是一項大任務。

kind.english: a type of person or thing

examples.english: What kind of music do you like?
examples.chinese: 你喜歡哪一種類的音樂？

examples.english: She is very kind to children.

land.english: the solid surface of the earth, not sea

examples.english: We could see land from the boat.

land.english: ground used for farming, building, or ownership

examples.english: They bought a piece of land.

land.english: to come down to the ground or make a plane come down

late.english: arriving or happening after the expected time

examples.english: Her late husband was a teacher.
examples.chinese: 她已故的丈夫是一名教師。

late.english: after the expected or usual time

examples.english: There is a mark on the wall.

mark.english: a score given for schoolwork or an exam
to put a sign on something

examples.english: Mark the date on your calendar.
examples.chinese: 在日曆上標記這個日期。

sound.english: to seem a particular way when heard or described
in good condition or based on good judgement

try.english: to make an effort to do something
to use or test something to see if it is good

examples.chinese: 我可以試穿這件外套嗎？

able.english: having the power or skill to do something
skilful or good at doing things

care.english: the process of looking after someone or something
serious attention to avoid harm or mistakes
to feel that something is important

examples.english: I am certain he will come.

certain.english: particular but not named or described exactly

cover.english: to put something over or on top of something

examples.english: Cover the pot with a lid.

cover.english: to include or deal with a subject
the outside front of a book or something that protects an object

examples.chinese: 這本書的封面是藍色的。

develop.english: to grow, change, or make something more advanced
to create a new product, idea, or system

examples.chinese: 他們研發了一個新應用程式。

during.english: throughout or at some time in a period

feel.english: to experience an emotion or physical sensation
to touch something or have a particular texture

examples.english: Feel how soft this cloth is.
examples.chinese: 觸摸看看這塊布多柔軟。

feel.english: the quality or impression something gives

examples.english: This room has a warm feel.
examples.chinese: 這個房間有溫暖的感覺。

kid.english: to joke or trick someone playfully

later.english: at a time in the future

mind.english: the part of a person that thinks and feels
to be annoyed or worried by something
to take care of or pay attention to something

once.english: one time or at some time in the past

examples.english: The start of the film was slow.
She gave a talk on health.
examples.chinese: 她做了一場關於健康的演講。

watch.english: to look at someone or something for a period of time
to pay attention to something carefully
a small clock worn on the wrist
the act of watching for danger or change

examples.english: The guard kept watch all night.
